name: Tadalafil Alpha-Blocker Hemodynamic Interaction Extract
population_or_context: Healthy normotensive men in two separate alpha-blocker interaction studies.
methods: Double-blind placebo-controlled randomized crossover studies evaluating doxazosin 8 mg with tadalafil 20 mg and tamsulosin 0.4 mg with tadalafil 10 or 20 mg, with blood pressure and heart rate recorded for 24 hours after dosing.
population_profile: Healthy normotensive men; direct for mostly healthy male hemodynamic interaction but not for patients with unstable blood pressure, dehydration, multiple antihypertensives, or BPH treatment combinations.
tadalafil_exposure: Single tadalafil 20 mg with doxazosin 8 mg; tadalafil 10 or 20 mg with tamsulosin 0.4 mg.
indication_context: Clinical pharmacology interaction study, not off-label enhancement use.
study_design: Two double-blind placebo-controlled randomized crossover studies.
safety_findings: Tadalafil augmented doxazosin-related standing systolic blood-pressure lowering and produced more standing SBP below 85 mm Hg outliers; tadalafil had little hemodynamic interaction with tamsulosin in the abstract.
directness_to_target_population: Moderate to high for healthy men age 30 to 50 taking alpha-blockers because the subjects were healthy men, but exposure and sample size are limited.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Small studies; abstract-only extraction; healthy normotensive participants do not capture older BPH patients, antihypertensive polypharmacy, alcohol use, volume depletion, or autonomic dysfunction.
